rationale: Pre-merge checks gate, bypass annotation, and e2e tests are correctly implemented and well-structured. AC #7 is not strictly satisfied: commit was made with lefthook --no-verify due to a pre-existing TestReviewEvidenceApproveAttributesToTier failure, which the author documented as reproducible on base 6a64e425. Implementation packages pass green; pre-existing failure should be filed as a separate bead.

findings.summary: AC #1 satisfied: RunPreMergeChecks delegates to checks.Run which filters by AppliesTo (cli/internal/checks/runner.go) and parallelises via sync.WaitGroup.

AC #2 satisfied: PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks writes refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/... via PreserveRef + UpdateRef, returns LandResult{Status:"preserved"}; covered by TestSubmitWithPreMergeChecks_DummyFailAbortsMerge asserting the ref resolves to resultSHA.

AC #3 satisfied: ChecksBypasses validates non-empty trimmed name+reason; SubmitWithPreMergeChecks treats setup errors (including missing-reason rejections) as a hard preserve so a misconfigured bypass cannot silently merge.

AC #4/#5/#6 satisfied: TestSubmitWithPreMergeChecks_{DummyFailAbortsMerge,BypassAllowsMerge,BypassMissingReasonRejected} drive a real git repo + bead store and assert preserve ref, evidence JSON file, submit-not-called/called, and event records on the bead.

AC #7 not satisfied as written: commit used --no-verify because lefthook go-test fails on TestReviewEvidenceApproveAttributesToTier. Author documents reproduction on base 6a64e425 (pre-existing, unrelated to this slice). The implementation packages (internal/agent, internal/bead, internal/checks) are green per the same evidence note. File a separate bead for the review-evidence test bug rather than absorbing it here.
